_meta.models: A=DeepSeek all-CSA (ffn565), C=CSA+GDN-2 (ffn862), 10M body, from ckpts_10m/*_final.pt
_meta.measure: PREFILL: full-sequence forward-pass latency (cache-free). No incremental decode-with-cache path exists (GDN-2 layer is training-only, use_cache=False).
_meta.why_not_llama_cpp: CSA and GDN-2 are custom architectures not supported by llama.cpp/GGUF; quantizing to GGUF would require a custom C++ backend for both layers.
_meta.conclusion: C is faster at every context length; advantage grows with context (x1.20 @512 -> x1.74 @4096). A (all-CSA) prefill latency bends up sharply past 2048 (56.8->99.2ms), C stays near-linear (47.5->57.0ms) - the linear-attention scaling signature.
